proposals.old_exact: [¶8] According the referee's analysis was based on
proposals.new_exact: [¶8] According to the report, the referee's analysis was based on
proposals.source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND206.pdf
proposals.evidence: PDF section ¶8 reads 'According to the report, the referee's analysis was based on'. DB text has incomplete phrase 'According the' missing 'to the report,' which belongs at the start of ¶8. The DB improperly merged content and dropped the phrase 'to the report,' from the sentence structure.
proposals.note: This is an OCR/ingestion defect. The phrase 'to the report,' was placed at the end of ¶7's long block quote (where it does not belong) and 'According the' in ¶8 is an incomplete phrase. The correct structure has ¶7 ending with the closing quote and ¶8 beginning with the full phrase 'According to the report, the referee's analysis...'

proposals.old_exact: comprise the subject property." to the report,
proposals.new_exact: comprise the subject property."
proposals.source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND206.pdf
proposals.evidence: The phrase 'to the report,' is misplaced in the DB at the end of ¶7's block quote. The PDF shows ¶7 ends with the closing curly quote after 'subject property.' with no 'to the report,' text. The phrase 'to the report,' belongs as part of ¶8's opening sentence 'According to the report, the referee's analysis...' (see Proposal 1).
proposals.note: This is part of the same defect as Proposal 1: the phrase was incorrectly split and relocated. Removing it here and restoring it to ¶8's opening (Proposal 1) corrects the structural error.

flags.location_quote: Corrected Opinion Filed 12/18/24 by Clerk of the Supreme Court (line 32 in DB, line 1 in PDF)
flags.description: Running header placement: In the PDF, this line appears at the very top as page furniture/court notation. In the DB text, it appears after the caption and before the opinion body. This is expected and correct per FORM-FEED rules—running headers in digitized opinions are handled by deterministic pass. No action needed.

flags.location_quote: Filed by Clerk of Supreme Court 12/18/24 (appears mid-opinion at ¶21/¶22 boundary)
flags.description: Mid-text filing notation: The PDF shows this timestamp line embedded between ¶21 and ¶22 (page 8 footer). The DB places it directly after ¶21 with no line break before [¶22]. This is a known page-break artifact and is handled by deterministic pass. No corrective action needed.
